canonical_name: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABHA Health Account)

exclusion_rules.description: ABHA enrollment is open to all residents of India. There is no income, occupation, age, or category bar — it is universal digital-health infrastructure, not a benefit scheme. ABHA can also be created for children under 18.

family_criteria.family_definition: ABHA is an individual health account — every person, including children under 18, gets their own 14-digit ABHA number. It is not a family-floater benefit; each family member enrolls separately.

covered_procedures: Creation of a unique 14-digit ABHA number (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) — free of cost
ABHA address (e.g. name@abdm) for sharing health records digitally
Linking and storing of personal health records (prescriptions, lab reports, discharge summaries) in a digital locker
Consent-based sharing of health records with doctors and hospitals
Access to the Health Facility Registry (HFR) of verified hospitals/clinics
Access to the Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR) of verified doctors
Scan-and-share OPD registration at participating hospitals to reduce queue time
Interoperable, portable health history accessible across providers nationwide
Increasingly used as the digital identity layer to access PM-JAY and other scheme benefits
excluded_procedures: Hospitalization or treatment cost (ABHA is NOT health insurance and pays no medical bills)
Any monetary coverage — ABHA only stores and shares health records

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Aadhaar with a linked mobile number is the fastest route — ABHA is generated instantly via OTP and is auto-verified. Driving licence or PAN can be used as alternatives.

required_documents.name: Mobile Number
required_documents.notes: Required to receive the OTP for verification and to access the ABHA app and records.

enrollment_channels: Scan ABDM QR code at any participating health facility
Healthcare facilities and hospitals enrolled under ABDM
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit abha.abdm.gov.in or download the official ABHA app (or scan the ABDM QR at a hospital)
Step 2: Choose to create ABHA using Aadhaar (fastest, auto-verified) or driving licence
Step 3: Enter the Aadhaar/ID number and the OTP received on your linked mobile number
Step 4: Complete KYC verification — for children under 18 a parent/guardian can create it
Step 5: A unique 14-digit ABHA number is generated free of cost
Step 6: Create a memorable ABHA address (e.g. yourname@abdm) for easy record sharing
Step 7: Link existing health records and use the account to share records with doctors via consent
Note: ABHA is a free digital health ID and record locker — it does NOT itself pay for treatment. To get financial cover, also enrol in PM-JAY or your applicable state health insurance scheme.
description_for_embedding: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in September 2021, builds India's integrated digital health infrastructure. Its core component is the ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) — a free, unique 14-digit health ID available to every resident of India, including children under 18. ABHA is NOT health insurance and pays no medical bills; instead it lets a person store, link, and consent-share their personal health records (prescriptions, lab reports, discharge summaries) digitally across hospitals and doctors nationwide, giving a portable, interoperable health history. There is no income, occupation, age, or category eligibility bar — enrollment is universal and free. The fastest way to create an ABHA is via Aadhaar with a linked mobile number (instant OTP verification and auto-verification); a driving licence is an alternative. Each person also gets an ABHA address (like name@abdm) for easy record sharing, and can use scan-and-share for faster OPD registration. ABDM also maintains the Health Facility Registry (HFR) of verified hospitals and the Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR) of verified doctors. Over 68 crore ABHA numbers have been created. The ABHA number is increasingly required as the digital identity layer to access PM-JAY and other government scheme benefits, so creating one is a useful first step before applying for health insurance schemes. Enrollment is via abha.abdm.gov.in, the ABHA app, a CSC, or by scanning an ABDM QR code at a participating facility.
